summary.intent: Implement GridList adapter components for Leptos and Dioxus (tasks #398/#490), then progressively improve visual parity with React Aria, DnD functionality, documentation, and code architecture to enforce agnostic-first behavior ownership.
summary.outcome: GridList adapters were implemented for both frameworks with DnD, sections, load-more, selection behavior variants, and full showcase across 6 widget crates; implementation docs were split into a navigable workflow and published as PR #721 (under review). The main GridList implementation remains uncommitted.

repo: Adapter-parity lint (cargo xtask lint adapter-parity) only discovers tests named test_*.rs — files like grid_list.rs are silently skipped
E2E fixture category modules must be directory-backed: categories/<category>/<component>.rs with a mod.rs aggregator, not a flat category file
Framework adapter skills (.agents/skills/leptos/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/dioxus/SKILL.md) must be loaded before editing any adapter crate code
Post-implementation audit skill (.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md) must be invoked and all findings fixed before presenting results
cargo xtask spec component-deps and cargo xtask spec issue-deps --dry-run are required blocker checks before any adapter implementation starts
Dioxus EventHandler is not Send + Sync, so it cannot be captured inside agnostic Callback props — adapter-local bridging is required
The adapter preludes expose framework-specific re-exports; new prop-facing types (DisabledBehavior, CollectionDndEvent, DropPosition) must be added there symmetrically
GitHub project status updates require the PVTSSF field ID and option ID from project field-list, not just item ID
The spec-conformance test has a hardcoded expected Part list alongside the spec file — both must be updated when adding new anatomy parts
StoredValue in Leptos and CopyValue in Dioxus are required for per-instance values captured by multiple closures; failing to use them causes ownership panics
cargo xtask spec validate checks spec metadata; cargo xtask spec toc validates heading structure; both must pass for docs changes
The xtask e2e command requires an explicit --no-server --headed --port flags to keep the fixture alive for debugging

code.finding: Core agnostic GridList owns all behavior: layout/orientation attrs, disabled behavior, press-up vs press-down selection policy, hover/press/drop-target state, DnD drag-key resolution, reorder payload construction, section/header traversal, load-more suppression. Adapters must consume API helpers and render_blocks(), not recompute this.

Dioxus adapter bridges to agnostic core via use_machine; must not use EventHandler inside Send+Sync callbacks. drop event handler must read api.drop_target().cloned() first before computing event-hit-testing fallback, otherwise grid preview reflow causes snap-back on release.

Leptos adapter: render projection must invalidate on api.drag_keys() and api.drop_target() or DnD state changes won't trigger cell re-render. Browser drag image API gated behind #[cfg(target_arch = 'wasm32')] to avoid web-sys method not found on native.

Synthetic drag simulation must split into two execute calls: (1) dispatch dragstart and wait, (2) re-query the drop target from the live DOM after the framework re-renders from DragStart, then dispatch dragenter/dragover on the fresh reference. Stale element refs after rerender cause dragover to silently no-op.

E2E fixtures must pass dnd_enabled=true to GridList for persisted reorder via on_dnd_event to work. Without it, the visual drag preview moves but the agnostic machine rejects DragStart/Drop so no CollectionDndEvent::Reorder fires and the item snaps back.

Widget examples must not reimplement GridList behavior (drag-key resolution, drop placement math, can-drop checks). They should only hold demo item state and apply reorder from on_dnd_event callback. Duplicated policy was removed when render_blocks() API exposed ItemRenderState.

Adapter-level component work requires fully reading docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md plus all linked workflow files. The checklist in AGENTS.md is explicitly not a substitute for the full doc. Added requirement for browser-based counterpart review before planning.

New explicit rule: renderer-independent behavior (selection, DnD, layout, load-more, parity rules) belongs in crates/ars-components or shared crates. Duplicating it separately in Leptos/Dioxus is a workflow violation. Only DOM refs, event translation, observers, native browser APIs, and rendering belong in adapters.

workflow: React Aria / React Spectrum is the required primary counterpart for visual and functional parity review; a live browser inspection of the reference page must happen before planning, not after

Browser-based visual verification of DnD is unreliable via the in-app browser's evaluate() context because DataTransfer and DragEvent constructors are not available there; E2E Playwright path is the only reliable automation for HTML drag events
Before/after drop placement must be computed from pointer position relative to target element midpoint, not from item index; index-based logic causes first-position drops to land in wrong position

Grid DnD live preview must re-render cards before drop to communicate the insertion slot; on drop, commit must use the stored core pending target (api.drop_target()) not re-derive from the DOM event, because the preview reflow moves the drop element under the pointer

When multiple items are selected, dragging a selected item should drag the full selected set in collection order; drag image should represent the entire set using a hidden preview element referenced via drag_preview_id prop
Adapter tests, E2E assertions, and widget examples must cover every supported React Aria feature axis; unsupported axes must be documented as N/A with concrete reasons in the spec/PR body
cargo +nightly fmt must be run and checked after every code change because rustfmt line-wrap differences accumulate quickly across large generated file changes
Documentation should describe the reference preference order (React Aria > Ark UI > Radix UI > others) and require developers to cite which counterpart features were verified in the browser

friction: Sandbox blocked local server binding (127.0.0.1:5201, 4127) for E2E tests and dev server, requiring repeated escalation for every browser E2E run

In-app browser evaluate() context lacked DataTransfer/DragEvent constructors, making it impossible to synthesize HTML drag events for live page verification; had to rely on E2E Playwright path instead
Dioxus dev server served a rebuild overlay even after successful builds, making in-app browser visual verification unreliable throughout the session
Git commit hooks took 5+ minutes without any output, creating uncertainty about whether commits had succeeded; required checking git log after each attempt
cargo test accepts only one test-name filter at a time, causing repeated 'no tests found' failures when passing two filter patterns

Synthetic E2E dragover events silently no-op when the target element reference becomes stale after the framework re-renders from DragStart state change; required splitting into two execute() calls with a DOM re-query between them
Drop events land on the preview item after grid reflows during drag, causing the drop handler to derive a wrong target; required using api.drop_target() pending state instead of event-hit-testing
Leptos view! macro parser rejects turbofish syntax (collection::<Type>::new()) inside prop expressions; requires moving these into helper bindings first
Dioxus rsx! macro requires event attributes before children/spreads; ordering violations produce cryptic parse errors
dx serve has interactive vs non-interactive mode issues and port binding failures in this environment; --interactive false was required for reliable background serving
open_items: cargo xtask e2e data-display --adapter dioxus grid live-preview reflow assertion was failing at session end due to synthetic dragover not reaching the DOM target after Dioxus re-renders; more investigation needed on the correct event dispatch sequence
Checkbox adapter component deliberately deferred to a separate session/branch; GridList selection visual indicators currently use ad-hoc checkbox markup

GridList still has React Aria parity gaps not yet implemented: real IntersectionObserver load-more callback, keyboard DnD, disabledBehavior full semantics, shouldSelectOnPressUp, sections/headers rendering from StaticCollection structural nodes
Utility E2E fixture category was only conservatively split into utility/showcase.rs instead of per-component files; should be split further when individual primitives are added

GridList implementation code (adapters, tests, E2E, examples, core extensions) is entirely uncommitted in the worktree on branch codex/issues-398-490-gridlist-adapters; needs full validation suite pass before commit

Docs-only PR #721 was under active Codex review at session end; final review pass with TypedChildren rule, cross-adapter E2E parity language, and clone-guidance reference fixes was pushed but not yet approved

The dioxus-web crate was added as an optional dependency under the web feature in ars-dioxus to enable native DataTransfer.set_drag_image; this dependency addition should be verified in the workspace Cargo.toml
Temporary E2E debug diagnostics (grid_list_debug_dump function) were left in crates/ars-e2e/src/data_display/grid_list.rs and should be removed before the final PR commit
